item.title: Iran says return to war unlikely, Trump ‘not satisfied’u with deal
item.description: Iran said that a return to war with the United States was unlikely as the two sides inched towards a deal, though Donald Trump said that he was not happy with Tehran’s latest offer…...

item.title: 500 families in Lalmonirhat celebrate Eid-ul-Azha in Line with Saudi Arabia
item.description: More than 500 families in three villages of Kaliganj Upazila in Lalmonirhat celebrated the holy Eid-ul-Azha on Wednesday in line with Saudi Arabia and other Middle Eastern countries....

item.title: Paturia-Daulatdia ferry services resume after storm suspension
item.description: Ferry services on the Aricha-Kazirhat and Paturia-Daulatdia river routes resumed on Wednesday morning after being suspended for nearly one hour due to stormy weather conditions....

item.title: United Medical College Hospital extends specialised services for measles patients
item.description: In response to the ongoing measles outbreak in Bangladesh, United Medical College Hospital has launched a dedicated measles management system to provide specialised care for infected children while ensuring the safety of patients on other hospital floors through strict infection-control measures and WHO-guided protocols, said a press release....

item.title: Eid-ul-Azha to be celebrated across Bangladesh on Thursday
item.description: Eid-ul-Azha, one of the biggest religious festivals of Muslims, will be celebrated across Bangladesh on Thursday with due religious solemnity and festivity.......

item.title: ‘Donald Trump’ buffalo not to be sacrificed: police
item.description: The police on Wednesday took into custody a pink-coloured albino buffalo named ‘Donald Trump’ on Wednesday afternoon, a day before Eid-ul-Azha.......

item.title: Nurunnahar Nimni becomes 3rd Bangladeshi woman to summit Everest
item.description: Bangladeshi mountaineer Nurunnahar Nimni has become the third Bangladeshi woman to successfully summit Mount Everest, the world’s highest peak.......

item.title: Heavy rain inundates Dhaka streets, hampers Eid preparations
item.description: Dhaka witnessed another spell of heavy rain accompanied by thunderstorms on Wednesday morning, disrupting Eid-ul-Azha preparations and causing water stagnation in several parts of the capital....

item.title: Man dies after falling from level-7 of Bashundhara Shopping Complex
item.description: A man died after falling from Level-7 of Bashundhara City Shopping Complex in the capital’s Panthapath area on Wednesday afternoon, the police said.......

item.title: India says 10 sailors released by Iranian authorities
item.description: Ten Indian sailors, detained in Iran in July 2025 on an oil tanker, have been released after 'sustained diplomatic engagement', India's shipping authorities said late Tuesday....

item.title: Ties with Belgium deepening steadily: Bangladesh
item.description: Bangladesh and Belgium have discussed the way forward to capitalise on the momentum created by the meeting at the political level for mutual benefit of the two countries.......

item.title: US commits to ending naval blockade in draft deal: Iran state TV
item.description: ‘The United States has committed itself to lifting Iran’s naval blockade and to cease harassing ships passing to or from the Islamic Republic of Iran,’ Iranian state TV reports......

item.title: Samsung workers approve bonus deal after big AI profits
item.description: Samsung Electronics union members on Wednesday approved a deal with management securing massive annual bonuses, averting a major strike as the South Korean chip giant's profits soar due to booming demand for artificial intelligence tools......

item.title: Five killed in two road accidents in Dhaka
item.description: At least five people were killed and 10 others were injured in separate road accidents in the capital Dhaka on Wednesday........

item.title: North Korea tests new multi-purpose missile launch system
item.description: North Korea said on Wednesday it had tested a new lightweight multi-purpose missile launching system and a multiple tactical cruise missile weapon system the previous day......

item.title: IMF confirms Bangladesh govt’s request for new programme
item.description: The International Monetary Fund has said that the Bangladesh government has requested a new IMF-supported programme as discussions continue over the country’s reform agenda and policy priorities.......

item.title: Daughter seeks Dipu Moni’s parole to attend critically ill father
item.description: According to the application, Dipu Moni’s husband Tawfiq Nawaz is in critical condition at United Hospital in Dhaka and her presence beside him is urgently required…...

item.title: Bengali filmmaker Anik Dutta dies after falling from terrace
item.description: Acclaimed Bengali filmmaker Anik Dutta died on Wednesday afternoon at a private hospital in South Kolkata of the Indian state of West Bengal after suffering critical injuries in a fall from the terrace of his wife’s residence near Gariahat, family members confirmed.......

item.title: PM Tarique Rahman greets country’s people on Eid-ul-Azha
item.description: Bangladesh prime minister Tarique Rahman on Wednesday greeted the countrymen and Muslims across the world on the occasion of Eid-ul-Azha.......

item.title: Iran rules out negotiations on enriched uranium
item.description: Iran’s Supreme National Security Council deputy secretary Ali Baqeri said that discussions on the fate of its enriched uranium reserves ‘are not on the agenda of the talks’…...

item.title: DSCC urges residents to use jute bags for sacrificial waste disposal
item.description: The Dhaka South City Corporation on Wednesday urged city dwellers to use jute bags provided by the corporation for the disposal of the wastes of sacrificial animals instead of indiscriminate disposal.......

item.title: EID JOURNEY: Passengers overcharged Tk 5.62 Crore in bus fares, claims PWAB
item.description: Passengers paid approximately Tk 5.62 crore extra on 26 bus routes across Bangladesh during the ongoing travel rush ahead of Eid-ul-Azha, according to findings released by the Passenger Welfare Association of Bangladesh on Wednesday....

item.title: Measles death toll climbs to 560 in Bangladesh, five more die in 24 hours
item.description: Five more children died from measles-like symptoms in Bangladesh in 24 hours till 8:00 am on Wednesday, taking the total death toll to 560 since March 15....
